{{Quote|Young master, have you heard the tale of the Jedi Master Murrtaggh and the Dark Underlord?|A Bimm bard|<ref name="HaR">''[[Heroes & Rogues]]''</ref>}}
In around [[1750 BBY]] during the [[New Sith Wars]], a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] known as the "'''Dark Underlord'''" reigned as a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. A shadowy figure of unknown origins, he was rumored to have been summoned from the realm of chaos. The Dark Underlord was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] who rose to leadership over the [[New Sith|revived Sith Empire]], establishing an alliance known as the [[Black Knights]]. Based on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Malrev IV]], the Underlord led a [[Blood/Legends|bloody]] campaign against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorizing]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. These activities provoked the ire of [[Murrtaggh]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who—aided by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]—misdirected the Black Knights in a [[Battle of Malrev IV|surprise attack]] against the Underlord's base of operations. This drove the Dark Underlord into a personal confrontation with Murrtaggh himself, during which the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] met his ultimate demise against the Jedi Master.

==Biography==
===Lord of the Sith===
{{Quote|Early in the last war, the Dark Underlord raised an army of Black Knights.|Restelly Quist's writings|<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>}}
[[File:80SithWar.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A leading Sith general of the Dark Underlord during the Battle of Malrev IV]]
The entity known only as the "Dark Underlord" was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual who existed as a [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] during the [[New Sith Wars]],<ref name="END" /><ref name="DSSB">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> a [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict initiated by the dark side-worshipping [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]] in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]]. During this time, the origins of the Dark Underlord remained an enigma, his true nature shrouded behind a cloud of speculation and Sith folklore. One popular theory tied his emergence to the actions of a lone [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith Acolyte]], who allegedly summoned the Underlord from the dark, [[Netherworld of the Force/Legends|netherworldly realm]] of [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]]. Another rumor claimed that the Dark Underlord was none other than the spirit of [[Xendor]],<ref name="END" /> a powerful [[darksider]] who—[[24,500 BBY|22,750 years earlier]]—lead the [[Legions of Lettow]] during the [[First Great Schism]] of the peacekeeping [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="jvs">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>

Despite his shadowy presence,<ref name="END" /> the Dark Underlord rose to prominence in around [[1750 BBY]], becoming a new leader of the [[New Sith|resurgent Sith Empire]] in a crusade against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and its [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] protectors.<ref name="TEA" /> Having seized the coveted mantle of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], he garnered infamy as a particularly [[Blood/Legends|bloodthirsty]] marauder, swathing through the frontline of battle and proving more than a match for the Jedi. Standing among the most powerful Sith of his time, the Dark Underlord organized his followers into a Sith alliance called the [[Black Knights]], forming a base of operations on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Malrev IV]].<ref name="END" /> Commanding his Black Knights, the Dark Underlord led an incursion into [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorizing]] numerous [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] along the [[Zona Miki route|Zona Miki hyperspace route]].<ref name="TEA" />

Reviled as a scourge of the Jedi and the Republic,<ref name="CSWE-UNDERLORD">{{CSWECite|I|169|Dark Underlord}}</ref> the Dark Underlord and his minions soon provoked the intervention of [[Murrtaggh]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who had been plotting retaliation against the Dark [[Sith Lord/Legends|Lord of the Sith]]. Unbeknownst to the Underlord, Murrtaggh enlisted a group of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] for a planned surprise attack against the Sith headquarters on Malrev IV. Unwittingly ensnared by Master Murrtaggh's [[Battle of Malrev IV|diversionary attack]], the Dark Underlord dispatched his Black Knights—[[Commander/Legends|commanded]] by the Sith Lord's [[Unidentified Zeltron Sith general|leading general]]—to eliminate the invading Mandalorian forces. This decision left the Underlord vulnerable, however, and allowed the Jedi Master to infiltrate Sith territory, resulting in a confrontation between the pair. Despite his power and deadly reputation, the Underlord met his match and fell at the hands of Murrtaggh, promptly ending the Dark Lord's reign.<ref name="END" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Though they fell in battle to the Jedi, the Black Knights have risen again and again over the centuries and may not be entirely silent even today.|Restelly Quist's writings|<ref name="TJP" />}}
The [[Death/Legends|death]] of the Dark Underlord had an immediate impact on Jedi Master Murrtaggh, who [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fell]] to the dark side upon slaying the Dark Lord of the Sith.<ref name="END" /> Despite the Underlord's demise, the Sith crusades not only remained undeterred, but grew more dominant over the following [[Standard century|centuries]] of the New Sith Wars.<ref name="TEA" /> Additionally, the Black Knights—although defeated—reformed their numbers on several occasions in the absence of their fallen master. Even following the eventual Sith defeat at the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|conclusion]] of the New Sith Wars,<ref name="TJP" /><ref name="TEA" /> the Black Knights remained active during a conflict known as the [[Fluwhaka revolt]]. Sometime after [[990 BBY]], the exploits of the Dark Underlord were briefly detailed in [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force|''The Jedi Path'']], a [[Book/Legends|guidebook]] intended for [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiates]].<ref name="TJP" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Dark-underlord.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Dark Underlord's true nature remained clouded in rumours, speculation and Sith folklore.]]
An emotionless dark side spirit,<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="DSSB" /> the Dark Underlord was an enigmatic figure during the New Sith Wars, leading to much speculation regarding his true nature and origins. Nonetheless, his reign as the Dark Lord was characterized by a penchant for carnage and plunder.<ref name="END" /><ref name="TEA" /> To this end, the Underlord adopted a hands-on approach as a Sith [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]],<ref name="END" /><ref name="CSWE-UNDERLORD" /> engaging directly in the thick of battle against the Jedi Order.<ref name="END" />

==Powers and abilities==
The Dark Underlord was a notably powerful figure among the resurgent Sith of the [[Draggulch Period]], a trait which not only helped him establish dominance over his Sith peers, This made him a singular threat to the Jedi.<ref name="END" /> As a melee combatant, the Underlord was a master of the dual-wielding [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat form]], brandishing a pair of [[Sith sword]]s in preference to the modern [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] of his time.<ref name="CSWE-UNDERLORD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Dark Underlord was first mentioned in ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] supplement released in [[1995]] for the first edition of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="HaR" /> In [[2001]], another supplement called ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' featured an illustration—drawn by [[Ashley Wood]]—depicting a figure described as a "Dark Side Spirit".<ref name="DSSB" /> This image was eventually [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] as a depiction of the Dark Underlord in "[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]," an article written by [[Abel G. Peña]] in [[2006]]. The article expanded upon the Underlord's backstory within the New Sith Wars,<ref name="END" /> particularly his ghostly presence as a dark side spirit, a role that Peña originally intended for a separate character known as [[Cronal|Blackhole]].<ref name="Pawns-3">{{Blog|abelgpena|83|The Emperor's Pawns Endnotes, Part 3: Blackhole and Revelations}}</ref>

{{SuccessionBox
|title=[[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]<ref name="END" />
|years=Circa [[1750 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" />
|before=Eventually [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]]<ref name="TEA" />
|before-years=[[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" />
|after=Eventually [[Darth Rivan]]<ref name="TEA" />
|after-years=Sometime between 1417 BBY and [[1250 BBY]]<ref name="EOTJ-Rivan">According to ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', [[Darth Rivan]] became a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] sometime between 1,150 and 1,400 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of the story. It can be deduced that ''Echoes of the Jedi'' takes place in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]; the story occurs two years after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which was declared in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. As such, the earliest time of Rivan's reign can be dated at 1417 BBY. Furthermore, ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' confirms [[1250 BBY]] as the beginning date for the rule of [[Belia Darzu]] (established as Rivan's successor in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]''), thus providing the latest potential date for Rivan's reign.</ref>}}
{{EndBox}}
